testcases.input: nums = [2,3,3,2], edges = [[0,1],[1,2],[1,3]] Output: [-1,0,0,1] Explanation: In the above figure, each node's value is in parentheses. - Node 0 has no coprime ancestors. - Node 1 has only one ancestor, node 0. Their values are coprime (gcd(2,3) == 1). - Node 2 has two ancestors, nodes 1 and 0. Node 1's value is not coprime (gcd(3,3) == 3), but node 0's value is (gcd(2,3) == 1), so node 0 is the closest valid ancestor. - Node 3 has two ancestors, nodes 1 and 0. It is coprime with node 1 (gcd(3,2) == 1), so node 1 is its closest valid ancestor.
